article_meta.title: Witness Giving Oath During TrialgettyWomen are not believed, one legal whitepaper finds

reasoning: The model has several significant issues: (1) It uses bias type labels not from the approved list (Confirmation Bias, Selective Evidence/Cherry-picking, Loaded Language, Statistical Bias, Framing Bias - none of these are in the defined list). (2) The first flagged item is inside quotation marks in the article - it's from a legal whitepaper being quoted. (3) Several items mix author prose with attributed content. (4) While the article does contain genuine author-level bias, the model's identification and labeling is problematic throughout.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: The first flagged text ('women telling stories of abuse by their male partners are simply not believed') is within quotation marks in the article, making it attributed source language from a legal whitepaper. The third item ('Denial of women's claims...') paraphrases Joan Meier's views - though the exact text appears to be the author's paraphrase/summary, it's closely tied to attributed content. The Amber Heard/Depp sentence is author prose but borders on presenting opinions attributed to a broader discourse.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Some flagged items are defensible as genuine bias in author prose (the Amber Heard framing, the 'unreliable narrators' absolutist statement), but the first item is a quotation mark violation and the statistical claim is a reasonable identification though labeled incorrectly. Overall mixed quality.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model catches several significant bias instances - the absolutist framing ('No matter the content...'), the selective use of the Depp/Heard case, the loaded characterization of courts. It misses some other instances like 'unprecedented wave' (loaded language), 'startling results' (sensationalism), and 'struggles they face coming forward to tell their truths' (with 'truths' in scare quotes suggesting author editorial stance). Moderate miss rate.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: All five bias type labels used (Confirmation Bias, Selective Evidence/Cherry-picking, Loaded Language, Statistical Bias, Framing Bias) are NOT from the approved list of bias types. The approved list includes types like Spin, Word Choice, Slant, Opinion Statements Presented as Fact, etc. This is a severe labeling failure - the model appears to have ignored the provided bias type taxonomy entirely.
